route.name: Southern Cabinets: Halfway to Happy
route.region: Thompson Falls / Thompson Lakes / Happy's Inn
route.description: Public GPX Montana overnighter with forest roads, chain-of-lakes camping, remote services, and bear-country logistics.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Montana overnighter with forest roads, chain-of-lakes camping, remote services, and bear-country logistics.
route.bestSeason: Late May to early October; July works with heat, smoke, and bear-aware planning.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Montana overnighter with forest roads, chain-of-lakes camping, remote services, and bear-country logistics.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Filter from lakes and creeks, noting small creeks may dry in summer.
resources.food: Happy's Inn is the midpoint resupply/meal option; confirm current hours.
resources.camping: Thompson Chain/Lakes campgrounds are key first-come/self-pay options; Happy's Inn has food but no camping/lodging.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Good for strong riders, not a casual overnighter.
Call Happy's Inn before relying on it.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Exact legal overnight availability for July 10, July 11, and July 12 is not proven; key camping is first-come-first-served.
Happy's Inn exact July 10-13 hours and non-concert overnight/camping permission are not proven.
The water plan relies on seasonal/filterable sources and a dry-camp carry; Thompson Chain rustic campsites have no drinking water.
Route-level road/access/fire/weather/smoke checks must be refreshed on July 9-10 before any next-weekend departure.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Reserve or otherwise document exact legal overnights for each July 10-13 night needed by the itinerary.
Call Happy's Inn and record July 10-13 store, food, fuel, and overnight-parking/camping availability.
Refresh Kootenai National Forest, Lolo National Forest, Montana FWP closures, mtfireinfo restrictions, AirNow smoke, and NWS forecasts on July 9-10.
Publish a no-service water and food contingency for the Vermillion climb and Thompson Chain dry-camp zone.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Seasonally plausible but long, remote, and bear-country; heat/smoke and Happy's Inn hours must be verified.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
